questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Sino-Soviet split led to the emergence of a multipolar world.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The Sino-Soviet split ended the bipolar world order and paved the way for a multipolar world.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Sino-Soviet split led to the emergence of a multipolar world as both China and the Soviet Union began to pursue their own interests and alliances, ending the bipolar world order dominated by the US and the USSR.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Cold War came to an end with the collapse of the Soviet Union.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The Soviet economy was unable to keep pace with the US and other Western countries, leading to its collapse.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The collapse of the Soviet economy due to its inability to keep pace with the US and other Western countries led to the collapse of the Soviet Union, marking the end of the Cold War.
